name: auth-patterns
description: Authentication and authorization patterns — JWT (access/refresh tokens, RS256/HS256, claims design, rotation), OAuth2 (authorization code, PKCE, client credentials, social login, OIDC), session auth (cookie-based sessions, Redis/DB stores, CSRF protection, session security), password security (bcrypt/argon2, reset flows, magic links, email verification, rate limiting), authorization (RBAC roles/permissions, ABAC, resource-level auth, policy patterns), and API auth (API keys, Bearer tokens, webhook signatures, scoped keys, auth middleware). 6 skills, 3 commands, 1 agent. No dependencies.
